• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Point-by-point response analyses: mutation position and sequencing-quality checks

Francesco Gazzo

2026-05-06

1 Aim of the analysis

This report documents the additional analyses performed for the point-by-point response.

The goal is to show, in a reproducible way, how the figures and statistical summaries were generated.

The report focuses on two main questions:

  1. Are mutations spatially enriched around the integration site?
  2. Do ALT-supporting bases show evidence of technical bias, such as proximity to read ends or reduced Phred quality?

2 Setup

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(openxlsx)
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
  library(broom)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(knitr)
})

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Load custom ggplot theme, if available
# ----------------------------------------------------------

if (file.exists(params$theme_file)) {
  source(params$theme_file)
} else {
  message("ggplot.R not found. Using theme_bw() as fallback.")
  my_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12)
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Output folders
# ----------------------------------------------------------

output_dir <- "point_by_point_outputs"
plot_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "plots")
table_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "tables")

dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(plot_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(table_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# Dataset order and colors
# ----------------------------------------------------------

dataset_levels <- c(
  "Cdkn2a-/- LV.SF.LTR",
  "Cdkn2a-/- SIN.LV.PGK",
  "WT LV.SF.LTR",
  "WT SIN.LV.PGK"
)

dataset_colors <- c(
  "Cdkn2a-/- LV.SF.LTR" = "#7fb3d5",
  "Cdkn2a-/- SIN.LV.PGK" = "#FF8C00",
  "WT LV.SF.LTR" = "#8FBC8B",
  "WT SIN.LV.PGK" = "#D98880"
)

3 Input files

This report uses two input files:

input_files <- c(
  params$mpileup_summary_file,
  params$phred_wide_file
)

input_files
## [1] "mpileup_quality_summary_per_mutation.xlsx"
## [2] "df_phred_wide.xlsx"
missing_files <- input_files[!file.exists(input_files)]

if (length(missing_files) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Missing input file(s): ",
    paste(missing_files, collapse = ", "),
    ". Please place them in the working directory or update the YAML params."
  )
}

4 Load data

df_raw <- read.xlsx(params$mpileup_summary_file)
df_phred <- read.xlsx(params$phred_wide_file)

data.frame(
  dataset = c("mpileup summary", "phred wide"),
  rows = c(nrow(df_raw), nrow(df_phred)),
  columns = c(ncol(df_raw), ncol(df_phred))
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Input dataset dimensions")
Input dataset dimensions
dataset rows columns
mpileup summary 1670 8
phred wide 1639 12

5 Required columns

required_raw_cols <- c(
  "ID_gene",
  "locus",
  "GenVec",
  "Mutation"
)

required_phred_cols <- c(
  "delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end",
  "delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred"
)

missing_raw_cols <- setdiff(required_raw_cols, names(df_raw))
missing_phred_cols <- setdiff(required_phred_cols, names(df_phred))

if (length(missing_raw_cols) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Missing column(s) in ",
    params$mpileup_summary_file,
    ": ",
    paste(missing_raw_cols, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

if (length(missing_phred_cols) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Missing column(s) in ",
    params$phred_wide_file,
    ": ",
    paste(missing_phred_cols, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

6 1. Spatial distribution of mutations relative to the integration site

6.1 Rationale

This analysis evaluates where mutations are located relative to the integration site.

The integration-site coordinate is extracted from ID_gene.

The signed distance is calculated as:

Definition of signed distance from integration site
variable definition
distance_from_is locus - is_position

Interpretation:

  • negative values indicate mutations upstream of the integration site;
  • positive values indicate mutations downstream of the integration site;
  • values close to zero indicate mutations close to the integration site.

6.2 Build mutation-to-IS distance table

df_distance <- df_raw %>%
  mutate(
    is_position = as.numeric(
      str_match(ID_gene, "chr[0-9A-Za-z]+_([0-9]+)_")[, 2]
    ),
    distance_from_is = locus - is_position,
    abs_distance_from_is = abs(distance_from_is),
    GenVec = factor(GenVec, levels = dataset_levels)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(GenVec),
    !is.na(is_position),
    !is.na(locus),
    !is.na(distance_from_is)
  ) %>%
  separate(
    GenVec,
    into = c("Genotype", "Vector"),
    sep = " ",
    remove = FALSE,
    extra = "merge"
  ) %>%
  select(
    GenVec,
    Genotype,
    Vector,
    Mutation,
    ID_gene,
    is_position,
    locus,
    distance_from_is,
    abs_distance_from_is
  )

if (nrow(df_distance) == 0) {
  stop("No valid rows available after building df_distance.")
}

head(df_distance, 10) %>%
  kable(caption = "First rows of the mutation-to-IS distance table")

6.3 Descriptive summary

distance_summary <- df_distance %>%
  group_by(GenVec, Genotype, Vector) %>%
  summarise(
    n_mutations = n(),
    mean_distance_from_is = mean(distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_distance_from_is = median(distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_distance_from_is = sd(distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_abs_distance_from_is = median(abs_distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_abs_distance_from_is = IQR(abs_distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_distance_from_is = min(distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_distance_from_is = max(distance_from_is, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_within_100_bp = mean(abs_distance_from_is <= 100, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    pct_within_1kb = mean(abs_distance_from_is <= 1000, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(
  distance_summary,
  file.path(table_dir, "distance_from_is_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

distance_summary %>%
  kable(
    digits = 3,
    caption = "Summary of mutation distances from integration site"
  )
Summary of mutation distances from integration site
GenVec Genotype Vector n_mutations mean_distance_from_is median_distance_from_is sd_distance_from_is median_abs_distance_from_is iqr_abs_distance_from_is min_distance_from_is max_distance_from_is pct_within_100_bp pct_within_1kb
Cdkn2a-/- LV.SF.LTR Cdkn2a-/- LV.SF.LTR 264 4.220 14.5 165.216 71 146.25 -520 598 62.121 100
Cdkn2a-/- SIN.LV.PGK Cdkn2a-/- SIN.LV.PGK 549 -2.916 -7.0 186.369 89 177.00 -714 524 54.098 100
WT LV.SF.LTR WT LV.SF.LTR 303 -6.020 -1.0 225.434 99 201.00 -688 599 50.165 100
WT SIN.LV.PGK WT SIN.LV.PGK 554 6.668 8.0 206.317 110 205.00 -539 610 47.834 100

6.4 Statistical test: is signed distance centered around zero?

For each dataset, a one-sample Wilcoxon signed-rank test is used to evaluate whether the signed distance distribution is centered around zero.

safe_wilcox_test <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]

  if (length(x) < 3 || length(unique(x)) < 2) {
    return(tibble(
      statistic = NA_real_,
      p.value = NA_real_,
      method = "Wilcoxon signed-rank test",
      alternative = "two.sided",
      note = "Insufficient sample size or variability"
    ))
  }

  broom::tidy(
    wilcox.test(x, mu = 0, exact = FALSE)
  ) %>%
    mutate(note = NA_character_)
}
distance_wilcox_results <- df_distance %>%
  group_by(GenVec) %>%
  summarise(values = list(distance_from_is), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(test = map(values, safe_wilcox_test)) %>%
  select(-values) %>%
  unnest(test)

write.csv(
  distance_wilcox_results,
  file.path(table_dir, "distance_from_is_wilcox_tests.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

distance_wilcox_results %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4,
    caption = "Wilcoxon signed-rank tests on signed distance from IS"
  )
Wilcoxon signed-rank tests on signed distance from IS
GenVec statistic p.value method alternative note
Cdkn2a-/- LV.SF.LTR 18894.0 0.2584 Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction two.sided NA
Cdkn2a-/- SIN.LV.PGK 75335.0 0.9674 Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction two.sided NA
WT LV.SF.LTR 22842.0 0.9033 Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction two.sided NA
WT SIN.LV.PGK 79798.5 0.4369 Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction two.sided NA

6.5 Statistical test: compare absolute distance across datasets

The Kruskal-Wallis test is used to compare the absolute mutation distance from the integration site across datasets.

distance_kruskal_result <- broom::tidy(
  kruskal.test(abs_distance_from_is ~ GenVec, data = df_distance)
)

write.csv(
  distance_kruskal_result,
  file.path(table_dir, "absolute_distance_from_is_kruskal_test.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

distance_kruskal_result %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4,
    caption = "Kruskal-Wallis test on absolute distance from IS"
  )
Kruskal-Wallis test on absolute distance from IS
statistic p.value parameter method
12.4103 0.0061 3 Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test

6.6 Plot: signed distance from integration site

p_distance_hist <- ggplot(
  df_distance,
  aes(x = distance_from_is, fill = GenVec)
) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 50,
    alpha = 0.7,
    position = "identity"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  facet_grid(Genotype ~ Vector) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = dataset_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  my_theme +
  labs(
    title = "Spatial distribution of mutations relative to the integration site",
    x = "Signed distance from IS, bp",
    y = "Number of mutations"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.background = element_blank()
  )

p_distance_hist

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "distance_from_is_histogram.png"),
  plot = p_distance_hist,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "distance_from_is_histogram.pdf"),
  plot = p_distance_hist,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)

6.7 Plot: absolute distance from integration site

p_abs_distance_boxplot <- ggplot(
  df_distance,
  aes(x = GenVec, y = abs_distance_from_is, fill = GenVec)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.35) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = dataset_colors, drop = FALSE) +
  my_theme +
  labs(
    title = "Absolute distance between mutations and the integration site",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Absolute distance from IS, bp, log10 scale"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1)
  )

p_abs_distance_boxplot

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "absolute_distance_from_is_boxplot.png"),
  plot = p_abs_distance_boxplot,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "absolute_distance_from_is_boxplot.pdf"),
  plot = p_abs_distance_boxplot,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)

7 2. Read-end and Phred-quality checks

7.1 Rationale

This analysis evaluates whether ALT-supporting bases show evidence of technical bias compared with REF-supporting bases.

The two main variables are:

  • delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end
  • delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred

Interpretation:

  • delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end < 0: ALT-supporting bases are closer to the nearest read end than REF-supporting bases.
  • delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end > 0: ALT-supporting bases are farther from the nearest read end than REF-supporting bases.
  • delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred < 0: ALT-supporting bases have lower mean Phred quality than REF-supporting bases.
  • delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred > 0: ALT-supporting bases have higher mean Phred quality than REF-supporting bases.

7.2 Build quality table

df_quality <- df_phred %>%
  mutate(
    delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end =
      as.numeric(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end),
    delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred =
      as.numeric(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end),
    !is.na(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred)
  )

if ("GenVec" %in% names(df_quality)) {
  df_quality <- df_quality %>%
    mutate(GenVec = factor(GenVec, levels = dataset_levels))
}

if (nrow(df_quality) == 0) {
  stop("No valid rows available after building df_quality.")
}

head(df_quality, 10) %>%
  kable(caption = "First rows of the quality table")

7.3 Descriptive summary

quality_summary <- df_quality %>%
  summarise(
    n_snp_mutations = n(),

    mean_delta_read_end_distance =
      mean(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end, na.rm = TRUE),

    median_delta_read_end_distance =
      median(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end, na.rm = TRUE),

    iqr_delta_read_end_distance =
      IQR(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end, na.rm = TRUE),

    pct_alt_closer_to_read_end =
      mean(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end < 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,

    mean_delta_phred =
      mean(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred, na.rm = TRUE),

    median_delta_phred =
      median(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred, na.rm = TRUE),

    iqr_delta_phred =
      IQR(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred, na.rm = TRUE),

    pct_alt_lower_phred =
      mean(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred < 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )

write.csv(
  quality_summary,
  file.path(table_dir, "quality_delta_summary.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

quality_summary %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4,
    caption = "Global ALT-minus-REF read-end and Phred-quality summaries"
  )
Global ALT-minus-REF read-end and Phred-quality summaries
n_snp_mutations mean_delta_read_end_distance median_delta_read_end_distance iqr_delta_read_end_distance pct_alt_closer_to_read_end mean_delta_phred median_delta_phred iqr_delta_phred pct_alt_lower_phred
1639 2e-04 0.002 0.0949 48.1391 -2.1064 -0.4801 2.9178 62.3551

7.4 Optional summary by dataset

This section is generated only if GenVec is present in df_phred_wide.xlsx.

if ("GenVec" %in% names(df_quality) && any(!is.na(df_quality$GenVec))) {

  quality_summary_by_dataset <- df_quality %>%
    filter(!is.na(GenVec)) %>%
    group_by(GenVec) %>%
    summarise(
      n_snp_mutations = n(),

      median_delta_read_end_distance =
        median(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end, na.rm = TRUE),

      pct_alt_closer_to_read_end =
        mean(delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end < 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,

      median_delta_phred =
        median(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred, na.rm = TRUE),

      pct_alt_lower_phred =
        mean(delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred < 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,

      .groups = "drop"
    )

  write.csv(
    quality_summary_by_dataset,
    file.path(table_dir, "quality_delta_summary_by_dataset.csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )

  quality_summary_by_dataset %>%
    kable(
      digits = 4,
      caption = "ALT-minus-REF read-end and Phred-quality summaries by dataset"
    )
}

7.5 Statistical tests on quality deltas

A one-sample Wilcoxon signed-rank test is used to evaluate whether each ALT-minus-REF delta distribution is centered around zero.

read_end_wilcox <- safe_wilcox_test(
  df_quality$delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end
) %>%
  mutate(variable = "delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end", .before = 1)

phred_wilcox <- safe_wilcox_test(
  df_quality$delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred
) %>%
  mutate(variable = "delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred", .before = 1)

quality_wilcox_results <- bind_rows(
  read_end_wilcox,
  phred_wilcox
)

write.csv(
  quality_wilcox_results,
  file.path(table_dir, "quality_delta_wilcox_tests.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

quality_wilcox_results %>%
  kable(
    digits = 4,
    caption = "Wilcoxon signed-rank tests on ALT-minus-REF quality deltas"
  )
Wilcoxon signed-rank tests on ALT-minus-REF quality deltas
variable statistic p.value method alternative note
delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end 684995.5 0.4185 Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction two.sided NA
delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred 380587.0 0.0000 Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction two.sided NA

7.6 Plot: ALT-minus-REF distance from nearest read end

p_read_end <- ggplot(
  df_quality,
  aes(x = delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end)
) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "ALT - REF relative distance from nearest read end",
    y = "Number of SNP mutations",
    title = "No enrichment of ALT-supporting bases near read ends"
  )

p_read_end

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "alt_minus_ref_read_end_distance.png"),
  plot = p_read_end,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "alt_minus_ref_read_end_distance.pdf"),
  plot = p_read_end,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)

7.7 Plot: ALT-minus-REF Phred quality

p_phred <- ggplot(
  df_quality,
  aes(x = delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred)
) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "ALT - REF mean Phred quality",
    y = "Number of SNP mutations",
    title = "ALT-supporting bases show a modest Phred reduction"
  )

p_phred

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "alt_minus_ref_phred_quality.png"),
  plot = p_phred,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(plot_dir, "alt_minus_ref_phred_quality.pdf"),
  plot = p_phred,
  width = 8,
  height = 5
)

8 3. Optional stratified quality plots

These plots are generated only if the GenVec column is available in df_phred_wide.xlsx.

if ("GenVec" %in% names(df_quality) && any(!is.na(df_quality$GenVec))) {

  p_read_end_by_dataset <- ggplot(
    df_quality,
    aes(x = delta_ALT_minus_REF_dist_to_end, fill = GenVec)
  ) +
    geom_histogram(
      bins = 60,
      alpha = 0.7,
      position = "identity"
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    facet_wrap(~ GenVec, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = dataset_colors, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      x = "ALT - REF relative distance from nearest read end",
      y = "Number of SNP mutations",
      title = "Read-end distance delta by dataset"
    ) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  p_read_end_by_dataset

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(plot_dir, "alt_minus_ref_read_end_distance_by_dataset.png"),
    plot = p_read_end_by_dataset,
    width = 9,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )

  p_phred_by_dataset <- ggplot(
    df_quality,
    aes(x = delta_ALT_minus_REF_phred, fill = GenVec)
  ) +
    geom_histogram(
      bins = 60,
      alpha = 0.7,
      position = "identity"
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    facet_wrap(~ GenVec, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = dataset_colors, drop = FALSE) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(
      x = "ALT - REF mean Phred quality",
      y = "Number of SNP mutations",
      title = "Phred-quality delta by dataset"
    ) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  p_phred_by_dataset

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(plot_dir, "alt_minus_ref_phred_quality_by_dataset.png"),
    plot = p_phred_by_dataset,
    width = 9,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

9 4. Output files

The report also saves the main output tables and plots to disk.

list.files(output_dir, recursive = TRUE) %>%
  data.frame(file = .) %>%
  kable(caption = "Files generated by this report")
Files generated by this report
file
plots/absolute_distance_from_is_boxplot.pdf
plots/absolute_distance_from_is_boxplot.png
plots/alt_minus_ref_phred_quality.pdf
plots/alt_minus_ref_phred_quality.png
plots/alt_minus_ref_read_end_distance.pdf
plots/alt_minus_ref_read_end_distance.png
plots/distance_from_is_histogram.pdf
plots/distance_from_is_histogram.png
tables/absolute_distance_from_is_kruskal_test.csv
tables/distance_from_is_summary.csv
tables/distance_from_is_wilcox_tests.csv
tables/quality_delta_summary.csv
tables/quality_delta_wilcox_tests.csv
